title: What do you want to know about beta-blockers? | CNN
text:
Source: CNN

For decades, beta-blockers have been commonly prescribed as a standard treatment for adults who have had heart attacks with no complications, and many people continue the medications for life. But agrowing body of researchhas begun to raise questions about that approach.

Should patients and their doctors be considering a change? What does the latest evidence show? What do people need to know about staying healthy after a heart attack?

Share your questions with CNN below.

See Full Web Article
